+# AI Dispatch Log
+
+- Timestamp: 2026-05-25T22:36:40.9662183+03:00

+- Issue: #186 - Persist automation timing traces as JSONL events
+- Issue URL: https://github.com/RustCADs/RGE/issues/186
+- Branch: $Branch
+- Loop exit code: $LoopExit
+- Codex control verdict: $Verdict
+- Loop log: $LoopLog
+
+## Process Trace
+
+1. Queue selected the oldest open ai-dispatch issue.
+2. Queue labelled the issue ai-dispatch-running.
+3. Queue created branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-186.
+4. Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1 ran Codex plan, Claude gate, Claude execute, and Codex control.
+5. Queue wrote this detailed log before staging, committing, merging, or pushing.
+6. If and only if exit code is 0 and Codex control verdict is pass, queue will fast-forward main and push origin/main.
+

+

+?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.meta.json
+~~~

+ Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 | 62 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++---
+ 2 files changed, 118 insertions(+), 6 deletions(-)
+~~~
+

+

+
+## Codex Control JSON
+

+{"verdict":"pass","summary":"Ready for queue commit/publish. The diff is scoped to the two allowed PowerShell scripts plus this dispatch's handoff artifacts. The implementation preserves the existing trace line, adds best-effort JSONL append only when tracing is enabled, writes under ignored .ai/dispatch-trace, and leaves no staged or trace files behind. The canonical verification log reports VERIFY OK for all 7 steps.","task_packet":"ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md","exec_packet":"ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300.md","changed_files":["Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1","Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.meta.json","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300.md","ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300.meta.json"],"required_fixes":[],"verification":[{"command":"git status --short --branch --untracked-files=all","result":"Branch ai-dispatch/ISSUE-186. Non-fatal global git ignore permission warnings appeared.","exit_code":0},{"command":"git diff --name-status && git diff --stat","result":"Tracked diff is limited to Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 and Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1, with 118 insertions and 6 deletions total.","exit_code":0},{"command":"git diff -- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","result":"Both scripts get the same localized trace-helper extension: lazy .ai/dispatch-trace JSONL path initialization, unchanged tracing enablement, original console trace output before best-effort JSON serialization/append, event extraction from existing message text, and repo/script/pid/timing fields.","exit_code":0},{"command":"git diff --check","result":"No whitespace errors reported.","exit_code":0},{"command":"Get-Content .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-186/verification.round0.log -Tail 20","result":"Canonical dispatch verification log ends with VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed, including fmt, architecture lints/tests, cargo deny, workspace tests, doctests, and bench no-run.","exit_code":0},{"command":"Select-String -Path .gitignore -Pattern '^/\\.ai/dispatch-\\*/|dispatch-trace|\\.ai'","result":".gitignore includes /.ai/dispatch-*/, which covers .ai/dispatch-trace/.","exit_code":0},{"command":"Test-Path -LiteralPath .ai/dispatch-trace","result":"False; no trace directory remains in the worktree.","exit_code":0},{"command":"git diff --cached --name-status","result":"No staged changes.","exit_code":0}],"commit_readiness":"ready_for_publish","commands_run":["git status --short --branch","git diff --name-status","git diff --stat","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300.md","Get-Content -Raw .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-186/verification.round0.log","git diff -- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md","Get-Content -Raw AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md","Select-String -Path .gitignore -Pattern '^/\\.ai/dispatch-\\*/|dispatch-trace|\\.ai'","Get-Content -LiteralPath Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 -TotalCount 180","Get-Content -LiteralPath Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 -TotalCount 170","Get-ChildItem -Force -LiteralPath .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-186","Select-String -Path .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-186/verification.round0.log -Pattern 'VERIFY OK|--- ok:|--- failed:|ERROR|FAIL'","git diff --check","git status --short --branch --untracked-files=all","Test-Path -LiteralPath .ai/dispatch-trace","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.meta.json","Get-Content -Raw ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300.meta.json","Select-String -Path Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 -Pattern RepoRoot","Select-String -Path Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 -Pattern RepoRoot","Select-String -Path Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 -Pattern repoSlug","Select-String -Path Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 -Pattern repoSlug","Select-String -Path Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 -Pattern Write-TimingTrace","Select-String -Path Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 -Pattern Write-TimingTrace","Get-Content -LiteralPath .ai/dispatch-ISSUE-186/verification.round0.log -Tail 20","git log -1 --oneline","git diff --cached --name-status"]}
+~~~

@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-186
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1 — Added Initialize-TimingTraceJsonl helper and extended Write-TimingTrace with best-effort JSONL append; console output unchanged.
+- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1 — Same extension applied to the queue runner's trace helper so child-process traces persist when env-var-inherited.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md — Source TASK packet.
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300.md — This execution report.
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` (around the existing trace block, previously lines 84-93): introduced four new script-scoped state variables (`TraceTimingScriptLeaf`, `TraceTimingJsonlPath`, `TraceTimingJsonlInitialized`, and a leaf-name constant), added an `Initialize-TimingTraceJsonl` helper that lazily creates `.ai/dispatch-trace/<leaf>-<timestamp>-<pid>.jsonl` only when tracing is enabled, and extended `Write-TimingTrace` to emit the original `Write-Output` console line first, then attempt one compact JSONL append inside a `try`/`catch` so write failures are swallowed.
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` (same trace block, previously lines 66-75): identical extension with the script leaf set to `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, so the child queue process emits its own JSONL stream when `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` runs it under `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_TRACE_TIMING` (the env var is inherited automatically; no `-TraceTiming` plumbing change was needed in the auto driver).
+
+JSONL contract per line:
+- One compact JSON object, `ConvertTo-Json -Compress -Depth 5`, terminated with `"\n"`, BOM-less UTF-8 via `[System.IO.File]::AppendAllText`.
+- Fields: `timestamp` (ISO 8601 with offset via `DateTime.ToString('o')`), `elapsed_seconds` (numeric `[double]` straight from the existing stopwatch), `script` (literal `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` / `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`), `pid` (numeric `$PID`), `event` (substring before first `:`; falls back to first whitespace-delimited token; falls back to full message), `message` (exact text passed to `Write-TimingTrace`), `repo` (`{ root, slug }` read from existing `$script:RepoRoot` / `$script:repoSlug` when set).
+- Trace files land under `.ai/dispatch-trace/`, which matches the existing `/.ai/dispatch-*/` ignore rule (verified via `git check-ignore`); no `.gitignore` edits.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added. Per Task scope (`MUST NOT add ... any new test file, fixture, generated asset ... or new scripts`), no automated test files were created. Behavioural validation was done by a transient `.ai/dispatch-trace-smoketest.ps1` standalone PowerShell harness that mirrored the new helpers verbatim and was deleted before the final verify gate; see "Verification Results" below for the three cases it exercised.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None. The task explicitly excludes docs.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+(See "Source" above — two files, single localized change block per file.)
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` (the exact gate from the Task Packet) -> exit 0; both files report `PARSE OK`.
+- `git diff --check` -> exit 0.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` -> exit 0, final line `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.` (69s wall on this machine). All seven mirrored CI steps reported `ok`: cargo +nightly fmt --check, architecture lints, architecture-lint test suite, cargo deny check, `cargo test --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo test --workspace --doc`, `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`.
+- Smoke verification (transient harness, then deleted) — three cases, all passing:
+  1. Tracing disabled (no `-TraceTiming`, no env var) -> no `.ai/dispatch-trace/` directory created, no JSONL file created, no console diff.
+  2. `-TraceTiming` set -> console emits the existing `[TRACE HH:mm:ss.fff +N.NNNs] $Message` lines unchanged, and `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto-<yyyyMMdd-HHmmss-fff>-<pid>.jsonl` is created under `.ai/dispatch-trace/` with one parseable compact JSON object per trace. Event extraction confirmed on real-shaped messages: `auto.tick: start (...)` -> `auto.tick`; `auto.halt-checks: done` -> `auto.halt-checks`; `queue.publish: ff-merge start (...)` -> `queue.publish`; `noColon firstToken-rest` -> `noColon`; `single-word-no-colon` -> `single-word-no-colon`. `pid` is JSON Int32, `elapsed_seconds` is JSON Decimal, `repo.root`/`repo.slug` populated.
+  3. Only `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_TRACE_TIMING=1` set (no `-TraceTiming`) -> same JSONL output for the Queue target, confirming the inherited-env-var path. ConvertFrom-Json round-trip preserves all 7 fields with the expected types.
+- Final diff scope inspection: `git status --short` reports only `M Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` / `M Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` plus the dispatch's own `?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_*` and `?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_*` artifacts. The transient `.ai/dispatch-trace-smoketest.ps1` and `.ai/dispatch-trace/` directory used during smoke verification were removed before the verify gate ran; nothing under `.ai/` is staged or tracked.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope. No new trace events were added; `Write-TimingTrace` continues to print exactly the existing message text via the existing `Write-Output` formula. The `Initialize-TimingTraceJsonl` helper is a non-trace one-shot initializer (no console output of its own) and is invoked lazily from inside `Write-TimingTrace`'s try block, so it activates only when tracing is enabled and only on the first trace call per process.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+- None. The trace path (`.ai/dispatch-trace/`) was confirmed gitignored by the existing `/.ai/dispatch-*/` rule before any write attempt, so no `.gitignore` change was required. The script-leaf string is a literal per-file constant rather than derived from `$PSCommandPath` so it stays deterministic even if the script were ever dot-sourced.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` modified.
+- Untracked items: `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md`, `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.meta.json`, `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_2026-05-25_22-30-27+0300.md` (this report).
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-186`
+- Last commit: `f02f37c` chore(dispatch): queue trace jsonl persistence
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-186
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,275 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-186
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300
+REVISION: 0
+RELATED_FILES:
+- Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1
+- Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_CORRECT_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_CORRECT_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #186
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Persist the existing opt-in automation timing trace stream as JSONL events for
+GitHub issue #186. `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and
+`Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` already emit human-readable trace lines when
+`-TraceTiming` or `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_TRACE_TIMING` enables tracing; this dispatch
+must add a best-effort machine-readable JSONL companion without changing the
+existing console trace text, dispatch behavior, publish policy, or retry
+policy.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- This dispatch's own generated handoff artifacts only:
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_TASK_2026-05-25_22-18-29+0300.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_*.md`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_*.meta.json`,
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_CORRECT_*.md`, and
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_CORRECT_*.meta.json`.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one. Do not manually create or edit unrelated dispatch logs.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `new-handoff.ps1`
+- `.gitignore`
+- `.ai/**`, except transient local JSONL trace files under an already ignored
+  `.ai/dispatch-*` scratch directory as described below
+- `ai_handoffs/**`, except this dispatch's own `ISSUE-186_TASK`,
+  `ISSUE-186_EXEC`, and `ISSUE-186_CORRECT` Markdown packets and matching
+  `.meta.json` sidecars listed above
+- `ai_dispatch_logs/**`, except this dispatch's own exact queue log artifact if
+  the queue runner creates one
+- Any dispatch loop prompt, Codex prompt, Claude prompt, control-review JSON
+  shape, queue issue-selection logic, labels, retry policy, recovery route,
+  publish gate, watchdog, preflight audit, scheduler behavior, or health
+  reporting behavior
+- Rust source, Cargo manifests, `Cargo.lock`, tests, fixtures, workflows, docs,
+  schemas, plans, status files, architecture-lint files, repository metadata,
+  or any file outside the explicit allowed edit set
+
+### MAY add new files
+
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_*.md` execution
+  report and matching generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_EXEC_*.meta.json`
+  sidecar.
+- This dispatch's own generated `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_CORRECT_*.md`
+  correction packet and matching generated
+  `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-186_CORRECT_*.meta.json` sidecar, only if a correction
+  round is required by the orchestrator.
+- This dispatch's own exact `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact if the queue
+  runner creates one.
+- Transient runtime trace JSONL files only under `.ai/dispatch-trace/` or an
+  equivalently existing gitignored `.ai/dispatch-*` scratch path. These files
+  are local diagnostics only and must not be staged or committed.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+
+- Any new source file, test file, fixture, generated asset, manifest, lockfile,
+  dependency file, crate, workflow, schema, script, doc, ADR, plan, status
+  file, architecture-lint artifact, `.gitignore` entry, or repository metadata
+  file.
+- Any handoff packet or sidecar for another dispatch.
+- Any `REVIEW` or `CLOSEOUT` packet for this orchestrator dispatch.
+- Any `ai_dispatch_logs/**` file other than this dispatch's own exact queue log
+  artifact if the queue runner creates one.
+- Any trace file outside an already ignored local scratch path. Do not create
+  `.ai/trace/` unless it is already ignored before this dispatch starts.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Extend the existing `Write-TimingTrace` helper in both
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` so every
+  existing timing trace call still writes the same console line and also
+  attempts to append one JSONL event when tracing is enabled.
+- Keep the current tracing enablement contract exactly: JSONL persistence is
+  active only when the existing `$script:TraceTimingEnabled` value is true,
+  which is driven by `-TraceTiming` or
+  `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_TRACE_TIMING=1|true|yes|on`.
+- Preserve the exact existing console trace output format and message text:
+  `[TRACE HH:mm:ss.fff +N.NNNs] $Message`. Do not rewrite existing
+  `Write-TimingTrace` call-site messages.
+- Write one compact JSON object per line. Each object must include these
+  fields:
+  - `timestamp`: event timestamp as an ISO 8601 string.
+  - `elapsed_seconds`: elapsed seconds from the existing timing stopwatch as a
+    JSON number, not a formatted string.
+  - `script`: the script leaf name, either `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` or
+    `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`.
+  - `pid`: the current PowerShell process id as a JSON number.
+  - `event`: the event name derived from the existing message without adding a
+    new taxonomy. Use the substring before the first colon when present, such
+    as `auto.tick` from `auto.tick: start (...)`; otherwise use the first
+    whitespace-delimited token or the full message if no token exists.
+  - `message`: the exact message string passed to `Write-TimingTrace`.
+  - `repo`: repo context with at least `root` and `slug` values when available.
+- Store trace files under `.ai/dispatch-trace/` unless the implementation uses
+  another path that is already gitignored before this dispatch. The directory
+  and files must be created only when tracing is enabled, and the trace
+  filename must be process-specific enough to avoid concurrent append
+  collisions, such as including the script name, timestamp, and `$PID`.
+- Make all JSONL directory creation, serialization, and append operations
+  best-effort. A trace write failure must not throw out of `Write-TimingTrace`,
+  must not change the script exit code, and must not block dispatch progress.
+- Keep the implementation compatible with Windows PowerShell 5.1.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The only source files changed are `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`.
+- With tracing disabled, neither script creates a trace directory, creates a
+  trace file, appends JSONL, or changes console output.
+- With tracing enabled through `-TraceTiming`, both scripts append JSONL events
+  for their existing timing trace calls.
+- With tracing enabled only through `RGE_AI_DISPATCH_TRACE_TIMING`, both
+  scripts append JSONL events for their existing timing trace calls. The auto
+  driver must not need to pass `-TraceTiming` explicitly for the queue child to
+  honor the inherited environment variable.
+- Existing console trace lines remain byte-for-byte equivalent in shape and
+  content to the current implementation for the same event messages. JSONL
+  persistence may not add warnings, banners, summaries, or other console trace
+  output.
+- Each JSONL line is one compact JSON object on one line, parseable by
+  `ConvertFrom-Json`, and carries `timestamp`, `elapsed_seconds`, `script`,
+  `pid`, `event`, `message`, and `repo` fields.
+- `elapsed_seconds` is numeric JSON. `pid` is numeric JSON. `message` preserves
+  the exact message passed to `Write-TimingTrace`.
+- Trace files are written only under an already ignored local scratch path,
+  preferably `.ai/dispatch-trace/` because it is covered by the existing
+  `/.ai/dispatch-*/` ignore rule. Trace files must not appear in the dispatch
+  commit.
+- If trace directory creation, serialization, or append fails, the script still
+  emits the original console trace line and continues as though JSONL
+  persistence were unavailable.
+- The implementation does not add aggregation, alerting, dashboards, taxonomy
+  labels, retry-policy changes, recovery routes, performance speedups,
+  watchdog changes, preflight changes, docs, schemas, Rust changes, Cargo
+  changes, tests, fixtures, dependencies, or new scripts.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is only for persisting existing automation timing traces from
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` as JSONL when
+  tracing is already enabled.
+- Do not add new timing trace events unless a tiny helper-initialization event
+  is unavoidable for correctness. Prefer persisting exactly the events that are
+  already printed by existing `Write-TimingTrace` calls.
+- Do not change timing, retry, publish, label, issue-selection, branch,
+  stash/restore, queue guard, orphan recovery, halt sentinel, or scheduler
+  behavior.
+- Do not change `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, including watchdog, preflight
+  audit, prompt, verification, control-review, or packet-finalization behavior.
+- Do not add any aggregate report, alert threshold, taxonomy table, status
+  dashboard, health-report integration, documentation, schema, or new command
+  surface.
+- Do not edit `.gitignore`. If the desired trace path is not already ignored,
+  choose a path that is already ignored rather than changing ignore rules.
+- Do not run a full autonomous or queue dispatch solely to test JSONL tracing;
+  avoid GitHub issue or publish side effects during verification.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- PowerShell parser validation for `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` and
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, expected exit 0:
+
+```powershell
+$files = @('.\Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1', '.\Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1')
+foreach ($file in $files) {
+  $tokens = $null
+  $errors = $null
+  [System.Management.Automation.Language.Parser]::ParseFile(
+    (Resolve-Path $file),
+    [ref]$tokens,
+    [ref]$errors
+  ) | Out-Null
+  if ($errors.Count -ne 0) {
+    $errors | Format-List *
+    exit 1
+  }
+}
+```
+
+- `git diff --check` is expected to exit 0.
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` is expected to exit 0 and report `VERIFY OK`.
+- Inspect the final diff and document that changed files are limited to
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, this dispatch's
+  own allowed handoff artifacts, and this dispatch's own exact queue-log
+  artifact if one exists. Local ignored JSONL traces must not be staged or
+  included in the commit.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt (without committing) and request fresh guidance if any
+of the following occur:
+
+- The requested JSONL trace persistence cannot be implemented by editing only
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, and this
+  dispatch's own generated artifacts.
+- Preserving the exact existing console trace output conflicts with JSONL
+  persistence.
+- JSONL persistence would run when tracing is disabled.
+- JSONL write failures would fail dispatch progress, change script exit codes,
+  or emit extra console output.
+- The implementation requires a trace path that is not already ignored, or
+  requires editing `.gitignore`.
+- The implementation would stage, commit, or otherwise publish trace JSONL
+  files.
+- The work requires adding aggregation alerts, taxonomy labels, retry-policy
+  changes, recovery routes, speedups, dashboards, docs, schemas, Rust changes,
+  Cargo changes, watchdog changes, preflight changes, dependencies, tests,
+  fixtures, or new scripts.
+- PowerShell parser validation, `git diff --check`, or
+  `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` fails for a reason that cannot be fixed inside
+  the allowed edit set.
+- Any edit outside the allowed file/artifact set appears. Do not clean or
+  revert unrelated user work; halt and report the deviation.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+Both target scripts already have the same trace-enablement pattern near the
+top: `$script:TraceTimingEnabled`, `$script:TraceTimingStopwatch`, and
+`Write-TimingTrace`. Keep the change local to that existing surface where
+possible. A small helper that initializes a process-local JSONL path lazily and
+another helper that appends compressed JSON inside a `try`/`catch` is likely
+enough.
+
+Use `ConvertTo-Json -Compress` or an equivalent PowerShell 5.1-compatible
+serializer to produce exactly one compact JSON object per line. If nested repo
+context is used, keep the conversion depth explicit. The `event` field is not
+a new classification system; it is a mechanical extraction from the existing
+message prefix so future tooling can group the same trace stream that humans
+already read.
+
+The safest trace location is `.ai/dispatch-trace/`, whose top-level directory
+name matches the repository's existing `/.ai/dispatch-*/` ignore rule. Do not
+choose `.ai/trace/` unless `git check-ignore` proves it is already ignored
+without changing `.gitignore`.
